zh.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan於1460年出生在法國諾門迪艾朋吉奧德莫。他和Lady Isabel Vermandois至少有1個女兒。他去世於1510年在法國i萊-德-福朗斯易維林斯姆奧藍--易維林斯享年50歲。

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Muelan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Caitlin.
Irish (Ulster): variant of   Mullen  .

variants.name: Mullen
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maoláin ‘descendant of Maolán’, a byname meaning ‘tonsured one, devotee’ (from a diminutive of maol ‘bald’). The variants Ó Maoileáin and Ó Maoilín result in a variety of vowels in the Anglicized forms.
Dutch (Van der Mullen): variant of Van der Molen (see Molen ).

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: North American: from the title originally adopted as a nickname parallel to Duke , King , etc. The title was used in England in Norman times as an equivalent of the French comte ‘count’ it is from Old English eorl ‘warrior, nobleman, prince’. In some cases the given name may have been taken from the surname Earl, which was originally either a nickname or a term denoting someone who worked in the household of an earl.

variants.name: Earle
meanings: North American: from the title originally adopted as a nickname parallel to Duke , King , etc. The title was used in England in Norman times as an equivalent of the French comte ‘count’ it is from Old English eorl ‘warrior, nobleman, prince’. In some cases the given name may have been taken from the surname Earl, which was originally either a nickname or a term denoting someone who worked in the household of an earl.

variants.name: Erle
meanings: Variant spelling of   Earl .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
meanings: Irish: An Irish name derived from Cornelius.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan was born in 1460, in Pont-Audemer, Eure, Normandy, France. He had at least 1 daughter with Lady Isabel Vermandois. He died in 1510, in Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan est né en 1460, à Pont-Audemer, Eure, Normandie, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Lady Isabel Vermandois. Il est décédé en 1510, à Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan wurde 1460 in Pont-Audemer, Eure, Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Lady Isabel Vermandois mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1510 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan è nato il nel 1460 nel Pont-Audemer, Eure, Normandie, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Lady Isabel Vermandois. Morì nel 1510, nel Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelanは1460年にフランスノーマンディエウアポント-アウデマーで生まれた。彼はLady Isabel Vermandoisとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1510年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスイベラインスメウラン-エン-イベラインスで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan(은)는 1460년 프랑스 노먼디 우르 폰트-오데머에서 태어났다. 그는 Lady Isabel Vermandois(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1510년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 이벨라이네스 우룰아--이벨라이네스에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan nasceu em 1460, em Pont-Audemer, Eure, Normandie, França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Lady Isabel Vermandois. Ele faleceu em 1510, em Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, França, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan родился в 1460 г. в местности понт-аудемир, эур, норманди, Франция. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Lady Isabel Vermandois. Он умер в 1510 г. в местности меулан-ен-ивелаинесь, ивелаинесь, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan nació en 1460, en Pont-Audemer, Eure, Normandie, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Lady Isabel Vermandois. Murió en 1510, en Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 50 años.
